title: ThreadDetailView — thread detail with hero and members
description: Create AggregatorApp/Threads/ThreadDetailView.swift. Initialised with `threadId: Int`. On appear: concurrently fetch `getThread(id:)` and the first page of `getThreadMembers(id:)`. Render in a ScrollView: (1) full-width AsyncImage hero when thread.imageURL is present (max ~220pt height, .scaledToFill, placeholder, graceful on failure, no layout impact when absent); (2) header — representativeTitle as .title3/.bold, metadata caption 'Updated {relative} · {sourceCount} sources', rollingSummary body, knownFacts as bulleted rows under 'Known facts' when non-empty; (3) members section split into active (suppressed==false) and suppressed (suppressed==true) — active ordered by publishedAt descending, each as a NavigationLink to ArticleDetailView(articleId: member.articleId) showing classification-label badge (using the mapping from the spec), cleanTitle or '(untitled)', and a source/date caption; suppressed members rendered under an 'Also covered by' subheading as compact sourceName links to ArticleDetailView. Paginate members with infinite scroll when nextCursor != nil. .navigationTitle(thread.representativeTitle) .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.inline). Handles loading, error, and empty states.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrently fetches thread and first members page on appear
Hero image renders full-width when imageURL is present; layout is unchanged when absent
knownFacts bulleted section is present when non-empty and absent when empty
Active members show classification-label badges using the full label mapping from the spec
Suppressed members appear under Also covered by as compact source-name links only
Each active member and each suppressed member link pushes ArticleDetailView(articleId: member.articleId)
Members paginate via infinite scroll when nextCursor is non-nil
.navigationTitle is set to the thread's representativeTitle with .inline display mode
Loading, error, and empty states are handled

handoff_summary.completed: ThreadDetailView.swift created and verified compiling. Implements: concurrent async-let fetch of thread + first members page on appear; full-width AsyncImage hero (220pt, scaledToFill, absent when imageURL nil); header with title3/bold title, metadata caption, rollingSummary body; knownFacts bulleted section; active members (suppressed==false, sorted publishedAt descending) as NavigationLinks with classification-label badges and source/date captions; suppressed members under "Also covered by" as compact source-name NavigationLinks; infinite scroll trigger on last active member onAppear; .navigationTitle with .inline display mode; loading/error/empty states.
handoff_summary.remaining: No remaining work for this bead. The tester bead should verify all acceptance criteria against the running app.
handoff_summary.risks: SourceKit showed false-positive "cannot find in scope" diagnostics for types in other files — build succeeded cleanly, confirming these were indexing artifacts. No runtime risks identified beyond normal network/API availability.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify all nine acceptance criteria against the running app on the simulator, focusing on: concurrent load, hero image presence/absence, known facts conditional rendering, active member badges mapping, suppressed members section, pagination via infinite scroll, and all three states (loading/error/empty).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: ThreadDetailView.swift is a new file — no merge conflicts expected. Sibling beads in the same worktree (e.g. ThreadPagerView, ThreadsView updates) may touch the same Threads/ directory but not this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. ScrollView + VStack approach chosen over List: the bead spec explicitly calls for a ScrollView; hero image edge-to-edge layout is cleaner without listRowInsets hacks. 2. async let concurrent fetch: both getThread and getThreadMembers are independent. 3. Members split at render time via computed properties. 4. Infinite scroll trigger on last active member onAppear. 5. Classification badge colors are semantic.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) concurrent fetch — both thread metadata and members appear together after initial load with no intermediate half-rendered state; (2) hero image visible when imageURL is non-nil, layout unchanged when nil; (3) knownFacts section appears/disappears based on data; (4) all eight classification label strings map correctly (new_thread→"New Thread", same_thread_new_fact→"New Fact", etc.); unknown labels fall back to raw string; (5) suppressed members appear under "Also covered by" header, not in the active members list; (6) NavigationLink from both active and suppressed members pushes ArticleDetailView; (7) scrolling to bottom of members triggers pagination when nextCursor is non-nil; (8) .navigationTitle shows thread's representativeTitle; (9) error state shows with retry button; loading state shows ProgressView; empty members state shows ContentUnavailableView.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Pagination trigger fires on last *active* member onAppear; if a page returns only suppressed members, the trigger won't fire until the user scrolls back and the last active member re-appears. This is an edge case for threads with many suppressed members and few active ones — acceptable for the current spec scope. ThreadPagerView (bead dependency) is not yet implemented, so ThreadDetailView will only be reachable via direct NavigationLink from a parent view until that bead completes.

conflict_risks: ThreadDetailView.swift is a new file — no merge conflicts expected. Sibling beads in the same worktree (e.g. ThreadPagerView, ThreadsView updates) may touch the same Threads/ directory but not this file.

execution_history.summary: Created AggregatorApp/Threads/ThreadDetailView.swift implementing the full thread detail screen with concurrent fetching, hero image, header section, known facts, active/suppressed members, infinite scroll pagination, and all loading/error/empty state handling. Build succeeded.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
